name: 21_Iris_Classification_SVM_Iris_ML
query: I request a project to classify iris species utilizing the Iris dataset with a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier implemented in `src/model.py`. The project should standardize the data in and perform feature selection in `src/data_loader.py`. It will document the classification accuracy and save it as `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt`, and generate and save a confusion matrix as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`. It will further create an interactive web application in `src/app.py` using Streamlit to showcase classification results and model performance, with the figures stored in `results/figures/`. The web page should be user-friendly, with a brief explanation of the model to help users understand how the SVM classifier works.

requirements.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.
Feature selection is performed to identify important features in `src/data_loader.py`.
A confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.
An interactive web application `src/app.py` is created using "Streamlit"` to showcase classification results and model performance in results/figures/.

preferences.criteria: The Streamlit web page should be user-friendly, allowing users to easily explore different aspects of the model's performance.
A brief model explanation should be included on the web page, helping users understand how the SVM classifier works.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The provided code does not show the Iris dataset being loaded or referenced. In `src/data_loader.py`, `load_dataset()` only returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and contains no import or call to `sklearn.datasets.load_iris` or any Iris file. `src/model.py` also has no dataset usage, and the trajectory evidence explicitly states there are no steps showing the Iris dataset being loaded, referenced, executed, or saved.

judge_stats.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` only contains placeholder code. The `load_dataset()` function returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and does not load or standardize any features. Although `build_transforms()` returns `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`, this is just a static list in a docstring-labeled placeholder and no actual standardization logic (e.g., scaler import, fit/transform, numeric processing) is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.

judge_stats.criteria: Feature selection is performed to identify important features in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` only declares the requirement text in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and provides placeholder functions. The actual implementation is `load_dataset()` returning `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and `build_transforms()` returning `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`. There is no feature-selection logic, no selection method/model, and no identified important features in the code or results.

<UNSATISFIED> `src/model.py` does not implement an SVM classifier. The file only defines metadata (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`), a `requirement_notes()` helper, and `build_model()`, which returns the string `"model placeholder"`. There is no SVM import (e.g. from `sklearn.svm`), no classifier construction, and no training/prediction logic in the provided code.

<UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)` rather than a saved classification accuracy value. The provided evidence shows no numeric accuracy result or formatted metric in that file.

judge_stats.criteria: A confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although a file exists at `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`, the provided evidence shows it is not a valid image: `"The image data you provided does not represent a valid image"`. Additionally, no trajectory or execution evidence shows any confusion-matrix generation or successful save action for this file.

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive web application `src/app.py` is created using "Streamlit"` to showcase classification results and model performance in results/figures/.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/app.py` does not implement an interactive Streamlit web application. The file only defines metadata (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`), a `requirement_notes()` helper, and a `main()` that returns a dictionary; it contains no `import streamlit`, no Streamlit UI calls (e.g. `st.title`, `st.image`), and no code that loads or displays artifacts from `results/figures/` such as `confusion_matrix.png`. The presence of `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` alone does not demonstrate a Streamlit app showcasing it.
